message:
feat(native_events): Cx-first run_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds NativeEventListener::run_with_cx behind the native-wezterm feature,
extending the Cx-first migration pattern already landed for
EventWaiter::wait_with_cx (event_stream.rs), WorkflowRunner,
SearchBridge, CautClient, SurvivalModel, MuxWatchdog, RestoreProcess,
TelemetryCollector, Tailer, IPC primitives, and the wait_for predicate
loop.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (+124, additions only)

   Adds `NativeEventListener::run_with_cx(self, cx: &Cx, event_tx,
   shutdown_flag)`. Behavior:

     * Pre-flight cancellation gate. If `cx.is_cancel_requested()` is
       true on entry, the accept loop is never entered and no
       connection tasks are spawned. An operator who has already
       abandoned the watch should not pay the accept poll / spawn
       cost.

     * Loop bound to the caller's Cx via
       `crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx`, so budget-driven
       cancellation from the outer scope cuts the accept poll wait
       deterministically under LabRuntime virtual time. Both the
       shutdown_flag and cx.is_cancel_requested() are checked each
       iteration so either cancellation path terminates promptly.

     * Spawned per-connection tasks remain independent; their
       `dispatch_event_with_timeout` path already uses
       `cx::for_request()` for its mpsc reserve (wa-k0tk5) and is
       outside this slice.

   The legacy `run` is preserved intact for callers that haven't
   migrated yet; this commit is strictly additive.

   New `run_with_cx_pre_cancelled_exits_immediately` test pins the
   pre-cancellation short-circuit on a real temp-socket listener and
   asserts a <1s wall-time ceiling so a Cx-unaware run loop (which
   would block one full ACCEPT_POLL_INTERVAL) would fail.

2. crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs (-7 +7)

   Pre-existing test compilation fix from commit 68f38d62: three
   references to `WaitCondition::Any` now use the actual variant name
   `WaitCondition::AnyEvent`. This unblocks `cargo test -p
   frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features --features
   native-wezterm` which was hitting E0599 on the stale variant name.
   Included in this commit because it sits in the same lib-test
   compilation surface I needed to exercise to land the
   run_with_cx test; separating it would require a preparatory
   commit that produces no behavior change.

   Also applies the rustfmt canonical layout rustfmt wants on a
   `Timeout { elapsed_ms: ... }` struct init and three
   `let restored: WaitResult = ...` binding splits. Pure formatting,
   no logic change.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features
    --features native-wezterm: pass

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features
    --features native-wezterm native_events::: 76/76 pass
    (75 existing + new run_with_cx_pre_cancelled_exits_immediately)

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features
    --features native-wezterm event_stream::: 47/47 pass
    (WaitCondition::Any → AnyEvent fix confirmed)

  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features
    --features native-wezterm -- -D warnings: clean

  * rustfmt --check on both files: clean

Bead: leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress — ipc.rs and
event_stream.rs already carry Cx-first entry points; native_events.rs
was the remaining local-transport surface under that lane's
inventory. No dependency graph changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
